name.description: The name of the class (e.g., '10th Grade Math').

Student.title: Student
Student.description: Represents a student in the school system.

number.description: The student's unique number within the class.

password.description: The student's password.  Note: Secure password handling should be implemented (hashing/salting). This system is not responsible for authentication.

needsPasswordChange.description: Indicates if the student needs to change their password on next login.

risks.description: References to RiskFactors. (Relationship: RiskFactor N:N Student)

projectPreferences.description: References to Lessons representing the student's project preferences. (Relationship: Student 1:N Lesson Preferences)

assignedLesson.description: Reference to Lesson. The lesson assigned to the student. (Relationship: Lesson 1:N Student)

name.description: The name of the lesson (e.g., 'Math Project').

quota.description: The maximum number of students allowed for this lesson.

RiskFactor.title: RiskFactor
RiskFactor.description: Represents a risk factor that can be associated with a student.

id.description: Unique identifier for the risk factor.

label.description: The label of the risk factor (e.g., 'Broken Family').

weight.description: The weight or severity of the risk factor.

InfoForm.title: InfoForm
InfoForm.description: Represents a detailed information form for a student.

Message.title: Message
Message.description: Represents a message exchanged between users.

senderId.description: Reference to User sending the message. (Relationship: User 1:N Message (Sent))

receiverId.description: Reference to User receiving the message. (Relationship: User 1:N Message (Received))

timestamp.description: The timestamp of when the message was sent.

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the application (either a teacher or a student). Authentication details are not stored here.

TeacherProfile.title: TeacherProfile
TeacherProfile.description: Represents the profile information for a teacher.

schoolName.description: The name of the school the teacher works at.

principalName.description: The name of the school's principal.

Grade.title: Grade
Grade.description: Represents a student's grades for a specific term.

definition.description: Stores class information. Includes class ID.

params.name: classId
params.description: The unique identifier of the class.

definition.description: Stores student data. Includes denormalized 'classId' for authorization independence.

params.name: classId
params.description: The unique identifier of the class the student belongs to.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the student.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores teacher profile information. Path-based ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the teacher.

params.name: lessonId
params.description: The unique identifier of the lesson.

params.name: riskFactorId
params.description: The unique identifier of the risk factor.

definition.description: Stores student info forms. Path-based ownership.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the student.

definition.description: Stores messages between users. Sender and receiver IDs are included.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier of the message.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the student.

definition.description: Indicates administrative privileges for a given user. Existence-based authorization.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

firestore.reasoning:
This Firestore structure prioritizes authorization independence and simplifies security rules.  The core principle is to avoid `get()` calls in rules by denormalizing authorization data. It also provides structural segregation based on access needs.

*   `/classes`: Stores class information.  No specific authorization concerns.
*   `/classes/{classId}/students`:  Stores student data. Authorization independence is achieved by storing `classId` within the student document itself, instead of relying on a parent-child relationship.  This allows direct querying and updating of students without needing to fetch the parent class.  This structure supports the QAP (Rules are not filters) since `list` operations on students will only return students within the scope of the authenticated user or a globally authorized role.
*   `/users/{userId}`: Stores generic user information.
*   `/users/{userId}/teacherProfile`:  Stores the profile information for teachers.  This is a path-based ownership model. Supports QAPs because a teacher can only query or modify their own profile.
*   `/lessons`:  Stores lesson data.  No specific authorization concerns.
*   `/riskFactors`: Stores risk factor information.  No specific authorization concerns.
*   `/students/{studentId}/infoForm`: Stores student info forms.  Path-based ownership ensures only the student or authorized personnel can access this data.
*   `/messages`:  Stores messages between users.  Sender and receiver IDs are included in each document.
*   `/students/{studentId}/grades`: Stores student grades. Path-based ownership, ensuring that only the student or authorized personnel can access this data.
*   `/roles_admin/{userId}`:  Documents in this collection indicate administrative privileges for a given user. The *existence* of a document grants admin rights. This is a DBAC approach and enables list operations without exposing sensitive data.

This structure minimizes the need for complex security rules that rely on traversing the database. Denormalization ensures that all necessary authorization information is readily available within each document. The path-based ownership model and dedicated roles collections further simplify security and improve performance.
